User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the AscendFolio application, including their profile information and linking to their portfolios and authored content.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification purposes. Authentication data is managed externally.

displayName.description: The publicly displayed name of the user, used for general identification and as an author name.

bio.description: A short biography of the user, particularly if they are an author. Optional.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture. Optional.

memberSince.description: The date and time when the user registered on the platform.

isPublic.description: Indicates if the user's portfolio is public.

role.description: The user's role, determining their access level.

Portfolio.title: Portfolio
Portfolio.description: Represents a collection of investment assets owned by a specific user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Portfolio entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this portfolio. (Relationship: User 1:N Portfolio)

name.description: A user-defined name for the portfolio.

creationDate.description: The date and time when the portfolio was created.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date and time when the portfolio was last updated.

Asset.title: Asset
Asset.description: Represents a specific investment holding (e.g., stock, ETF, cryptocurrency) within a user's portfolio.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Asset entity.

portfolioId.description: Reference to the Portfolio this asset belongs to. (Relationship: Portfolio 1:N Asset)

assetType.description: The type of investment asset, e.g., 'stock', 'ETF', 'cryptocurrency', 'bond'.

symbol.description: The ticker symbol or unique identifier for the asset, e.g., 'AAPL', 'BTC'.

quantity.description: The number of units of the asset held in the portfolio.

averageCost.description: The average purchase price per unit of the asset.

currency.description: The currency in which the asset is denominated, e.g., 'USD'.

purchaseDate.description: The date when the asset was first purchased. Optional.

AssetPriceHistory.title: AssetPriceHistory
AssetPriceHistory.description: Stores historical and real-time pricing data for individual assets.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AssetPriceHistory entity.

assetSymbol.description: The ticker symbol or unique identifier for the asset this price history pertains to. (Relationship: FinancialInstrument 1:N AssetPriceHistory based on symbol)

priceTimestamp.description: The date and time of the price recording.

price.description: The recorded price of the asset at the given timestamp.

currency.description: The currency of the recorded price, e.g., 'USD'.

ContentPost.title: ContentPost
ContentPost.description: Represents a piece of published content such as an article, market analysis, or investment insight.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContentPost entity.

title.description: The title of the published content.

slug.description: A URL-friendly identifier for the content, typically derived from the title.

contentHtml.description: The main body of the content, stored as HTML or rich text.

authorId.description: Reference to the User who authored this content. (Relationship: User 1:N ContentPost)

publishedDate.description: The date and time when the content was published.

lastModifiedDate.description: The date and time when the content was last modified. Optional.

status.description: The publication status of the content, e.g., 'draft', 'published', 'archived'.

summary.description: A brief summary or excerpt of the content.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to a thumbnail image associated with the content. Optional.

categoryIds.description: References to categories associated with this content. (Relationship: ContentCategory N:N ContentPost)

tagIds.description: References to tags associated with this content. (Relationship: ContentTag N:N ContentPost)

ContentCategory.title: ContentCategory
ContentCategory.description: Represents a category for organizing published content.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContentCategory entity.

name.description: The name of the category, e.g., 'Market Analysis', 'Investment Tips'.

slug.description: A URL-friendly identifier for the category, derived from the name.

description.description: A brief description of what this category covers.

ContentTag.title: ContentTag
ContentTag.description: Represents a tag for granular categorization and search of published content.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContentTag entity.

name.description: The name of the tag, e.g., 'stocks', 'crypto', 'long-term'.

slug.description: A URL-friendly identifier for the tag, derived from the name.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile information. Accessible only by the owner (request.auth.uid == userId).
Publicly readable user profiles, keyed by username. Writable only by the owner.
Private collection of investment portfolios for a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, ensuring request.auth.uid matches the path's userId.

Private collection of investment assets within a user's specific portfolio. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'portfolioId' for authorization independence, ensuring request.auth.uid matches the path's userId.
Global collection for historical and real-time pricing data for various assets. Publicly readable by all users.
Stores content posts in a 'draft' or 'private' status, owned and editable only by the specified user. The 'authorId' field in the document matches the path's 'userId' for explicit authorization.

Stores content posts that have been 'published', making them publicly readable. Write access is restricted to the original author (identified by the 'authorId' field in the document) or an administrator.
Global collection of content categories, publicly readable. Write access is typically restricted to administrators.
Global collection of content tags, publicly readable. Write access is typically restricted to administrators.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes authorization independence, security, and debuggability, especially with the new publishing website feature. User-specific private data, such as portfolios and draft content, is nested under the `/users/{userId}` path, leveraging path-based ownership for simplified security rules. Global, publicly accessible data, like published content posts, content categories, and tags, resides in top-level collections.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
- For `Portfolio` and `Asset` entities, the `userId` is explicitly included in the document data, and `portfolioId` is included in `Asset` documents. This denormalization means that security rules for these subcollections do not need to perform `get()` operations on parent documents (e.g., to check if `request.auth.uid` matches the portfolio's owner). Instead, rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `userId` parameter in the path or the `userId` field within the document itself, making rules atomic and easier to debug.
- For `ContentPost` entities, structural segregation is used instead of direct denormalization within a single collection for authorization. Draft posts are stored in `/users/{userId}/draft_posts/{postId}`, making `userId` directly available in the path for authorization. Published posts are in `/published_posts/{postId}`, and while they contain `authorId`, read access is public, eliminating the need for `get()` calls for read authorization. Write access to `published_posts` can still utilize the `authorId` field for author-specific edits.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
- **Private Data:** Collections like `/users/{userId}/portfolios` and `/users/{userId}/draft_posts` enforce ownership through the `{userId}` wildcard in their paths. Security rules can directly match `request.auth.uid == userId`, ensuring that `list` operations implicitly return only data owned by the authenticated user without requiring Firestore to filter results post-query.
- **Public Data:** Collections such as `/published_posts`, `/content_categories`, `/content_tags`, and `/asset_price_history` are designed for global read access. Their security rules can be `allow read: if true;`, meaning any client can perform `list` operations on these collections and receive all documents. Write access to these public collections will be restricted, typically to administrators or the original authors (for `published_posts`), using checks against `request.auth.uid` and/or specific roles (not detailed here but would reside in a `/roles` collection).

**Publishing Website Feature:**
To support the publishing website, `ContentPost` documents are explicitly segregated by their publication status:
- Drafts are stored under `users/{userId}/draft_posts/{postId}`. This ensures only the author (`userId`) can read, create, update, or delete their own drafts.
- Published content is moved to a top-level collection `published_posts/{postId}`. These documents are globally readable (`allow read: if true;`). Write access remains restricted to the `authorId` stored within the document itself, allowing authors to update their published content, or to administrators.

This segregation simplifies rules, enhances security, and improves performance for querying public vs. private content.
